name: River to River
description_short: River to River is a public radio news and conversation program hosted by Iowa Public Radio, not a financial services or lending company.
description_long:
River to River is a nationally award-winning public radio program produced and distributed by Iowa Public Radio, a non-profit public broadcasting station. The program airs weekdays at noon on Iowa Public Radio's News & Studio One (FM) stations and online, featuring in-depth news discussion and community conversation from an Iowa perspective. Hosted by Ben Kieffer, the program is produced by a team including Caitlin Troutman, Danielle Gehr, Samantha McIntosh, Natalie Dunlap, and executive producer Katherine Perkins.

The program offers listener engagement through live call-in shows (866-780-9100), email interaction (rivertoriver@iowapublicradio.org), and podcast distribution across Apple Podcasts and Spotify. Content is structured around three recurring segments: Monday legislative coverage during session, Wednesday political analysis from state experts, and Friday news roundups. Episode topics span agriculture, healthcare, environmental policy, immigration, cancer research, and state government.

River to River distinguishes itself through its focus on Iowa-specific issues and local expert commentary, combined with national perspective (including NPR contributors). The program explicitly aims to "dig into the story behind the headlines" and "foster conversation and understanding" on challenging topics from multiple viewpoints. This educational, journalism-based approach positions it as community-focused information programming rather than commercial financial services.

This is fundamentally a public radio news program, not a consumer finance product. It has been miscategorized as a payday-alternative lender. The program provides no financial services, lending, credit products, or emergency cash services of any kind. It is educational public media content focused on news discussion and civic engagement.
services: Live weekday radio broadcast (noon on FM and online/AM)
Email contact for listener questions and feedback
Monday legislative session coverage and interviews with lawmakers
Wednesday political analysis segments with state-based experts
Friday news roundup and headline discussion (Newsbuzz)
Expert guest interviews on policy topics
Online streaming access through Iowa Public Radio website
pros: Free access to in-depth, award-winning news analysis and expert commentary on Iowa policy issues
Live call-in participation available weekdays noon-1pm at no cost to listeners
Multiple subscription and listening formats: broadcast, podcast (Apple/Spotify), online streaming, and email updates
Non-profit, public broadcasting model ensures editorial independence without commercial lending incentives
Expert guests including NPR correspondents, political analysts, farmers, researchers, and policymakers
Covers underreported rural and agricultural issues affecting Iowa communities
Structured recurring segments help listeners follow legislative session developments and political trends
cons: Not a financial services provider—no lending, credit products, or emergency cash assistance available
Only available to Iowa Public Radio listeners; geographic limitation to Midwest audience
Real-time participation limited to specific broadcast windows (weekdays noon-1pm)
Content is news discussion and analysis, not personalized financial counseling or credit guidance
No direct consumer finance support or emergency assistance capability
best_for: Iowa residents seeking free, in-depth local news analysis and public affairs programming
Listeners interested in agricultural policy, rural issues, and state legislative coverage
Community members wanting to participate in civic dialogue and public conversation on current events
People researching Iowa-specific news context (not for financial assistance or credit help)
diagnosis: River to River is a public radio news program, not a consumer finance company or lending service. It provides free news analysis and community conversation on Iowa issues, not financial products, credit assistance, or emergency loans. The categorization as 'payday-alternatives' is a critical error—this company should be recategorized or removed from CreditDoc entirely as it operates outside the consumer finance sector.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
